properties.headline: Flood Warning issued April 16 at 9:27PM AST until April 16 at 10:30PM AST by NWS San Juan PR
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall continues.

* WHERE...Along Rio Gurabo in Gurabo.

* WHEN...Until 1030 PM AST.

* IMPACTS...Elevated streams due to excess runoff from earlier
rainfall. Low-water crossings are inundated with water and may not
be passable.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 916 PM AST, USGS gauge reports indicated that Rio Gurabo
at Gurabo (GURP4) is in minor flood stage (20.35 ft).
Flooding is ongoing in the warned area. Between 1 and 1.5
inches of rain have fallen in the area.
- Sectors downstream of this river at Juncos and Las Piedras
are already under another Flood Warning.
- Emergency managers reported that road PR-941 intersection
with PR-942 along Rio Gurabo was completely flooded due to
the river out of its banks and impassable. Local emergency
managers have closed PR-941, km 0.9, towards the intersection
with PR-942.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
properties.instruction:
Turn around, don't drown when encountering flooded roads. Most flood
deaths occur in vehicles.

NWSheadline: FLOOD WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 1030 PM AST THIS EVENING

properties.headline: Flood Warning issued April 16 at 7:43PM AST until April 16 at 10:30PM AST by NWS San Juan PR
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall continues.

* WHERE...Humacao and Yabucoa.

* WHEN...Until 1030 PM AST.

* IMPACTS...Flooding of rivers, creeks, streams, and other low-lying
and flood-prone locations is imminent or occurring. Low-water
crossings are inundated with water and may not be passable.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 743 PM AST, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the warned area. Between 3 and 7 inches of rain
have fallen.
- Additional rainfall amounts up to 2 inches are possible in
the warned area.
properties.instruction:
Turn around, don't drown when encountering flooded roads. Most flood
deaths occur in vehicles.

Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.

Please report observed flooding to local emergency services or law
enforcement and request they pass this information to the National
Weather Service when you can do so safely.

NWSheadline: FLOOD WARNING NOW IN EFFECT UNTIL 1030 PM AST THIS EVENING

properties.areaDesc: Juncos, PR; Las Piedras, PR; San Lorenzo, PR
properties.headline: Flood Warning issued April 16 at 7:41PM AST until April 16 at 10:30PM AST by NWS San Juan PR
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall continues.

* WHERE...Juncos, Las Piedras and San Lorenzo.

* WHEN...Until 1030 PM AST.

* IMPACTS...Flooding of rivers, creeks, streams, and other low-lying
and flood-prone locations is imminent or occurring.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 736 PM AST, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the warned area. Between 3 and 8.5 inches of rain
have fallen.
- Additional rainfall amounts up to 1 inch are possible in the
warned area. Although the latest data from river sensors are
showing a decrease in water levels, hazardous conditions
continue along Rio Valenciano, Rio Gurabo and Rio Caguayas.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
properties.instruction:
Turn around, don't drown when encountering flooded roads. Most flood
deaths occur in vehicles.

Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.

Please report observed flooding to local emergency services or law
enforcement and request they pass this information to the National
Weather Service when you can do so safely.

NWSheadline: FLOOD WARNING NOW IN EFFECT UNTIL 1030 PM AST THIS EVENING
